title: lygodium heterodoxum
intro: Lygodium heterodoxum, also known as the climbing fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Lygodiaceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Its health benefits include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a diuretic. The herbal preparations of Lygodium heterodoxum typically involve drying and processing the plant's rhizomes and leaves, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
